title: eriogonum flavum subsp. crassifolium
intro: Eriogonum flavum subsp. crassifolium, also known as the yellow sulphur-flower buckwheat or yellow buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum flavum subsp. crassifolium include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves for topical application, making it a versatile and valuable plant for natural remedies.
